name: Peña Buraca Trail from Piedras Albas
name_original: Puente de Segura desde Piedras Albas — ida y vuelta
description: Hike 10 miles (16.1 km) from Piedras Albas to the historic Roman Segura International Bridge, a moderate round trip with 705 feet of elevati
region_intro: Hiking around Piedras Albas offers exploration of a landscape characterized by granite formations, traditional dehesa woodlands, and river valleys. Situated in Spain's Extremadura region, the area features the Erjas and Alagón Rivers, which contribute to its diverse natural environment. The terrain includes hills like Cerro del Castillo, offering panoramic views, and unique geological features such as Peña Buraca. This region is part of the Tajo Internacional Natural Park, providing protected habitats and varied walking conditions. Best hiking trails around Piedras Albas The most popular hiking route is Natural Park of the International Tagus – circular route by the Balcony of the World - Alcantara, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the protected natural park area. Another top favourite among local hikers is Menhir del Cabezo from the Mirador del Presa del Embalse de Alcántara — circular by Ruta de la Cañada Real de Gata, a moderate 8.3 miles (13.3 km) path. This route follows a traditional cattle trail, offering views of the reservoir and surrounding countryside. Local hikers also love the Menhir del Cabezo from Piedras Albas — circular via Ruta de la Cañada Real de Gata, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail leading through dehesa landscape, often completed in about 2 hours 26 minutes. Hiking around Piedras Albas is defined by ancient dehesa woodlands, granite rock formations, and riverine environments along the Erjas. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to moderate excursions. The routes in Piedras Albas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 12 reviews. More than 30 hikers have used komoot to explore Piedras Albas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Piedras Albas?
A: There are over 10 hiking routes in the Piedras Albas area, offering a variety of experiences. These include 6 easy routes, 7 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Piedras Albas?
A: Yes, Piedras Albas offers several easy routes suitable for families and beginners. A notable option is the path leading to &lt;strong&gt;Peña Buraca&lt;/strong&gt;, a unique granite formation with ancient rock carvings, which is generally accessible and enjoyable for all ages. Overall, there are 6 easy routes to choose from.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Piedras Albas?
A: Hiking in Piedras Albas will take you through diverse landscapes, including the classic Spanish &lt;em&gt;dehesa&lt;/em&gt; with its ancient oak trees and expansive pastures. You'll also encounter unique granite rock formations, riverine environments along the Erjas and Alagón Rivers, and hills like &lt;strong&gt;Cerro del Castillo&lt;/strong&gt;, which offer panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990823282/natural-park-of-the-international-tagus-circular-route-by-the-balcony-of-the-world-alcantara" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of the International Tagus – circular route by the Balcony of the World - Alcantara&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304682131/menhir-del-cabezo-from-piedras-albas-circular-via-ruta-de-la-canada-real-de-gata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menhir del Cabezo from Piedras Albas — circular via Ruta de la Cañada Real de Gata&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through the dehesa landscape.

Q: What historical or geological features can I explore on the trails?
A: The trails are rich in history and geology. You can visit &lt;strong&gt;Peña Buraca&lt;/strong&gt;, a prehistoric rock sanctuary with anthropomorphic tombs, or the ruins of the &lt;strong&gt;Castle of Piedras Albas&lt;/strong&gt; on Cerro del Castillo. The region also features remnants of an ancient Roman road and the well-preserved &lt;strong&gt;Roman Segura Bridge&lt;/strong&gt; over the River Erjas. Unique geological formations like white quartzite outcrops are also common.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Piedras Albas?
A: While specific regulations can vary, generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in the natural areas around Piedras Albas, especially within the dehesa landscape. It's always advisable to keep them on a leash to protect local wildlife and livestock, particularly in areas like the Tajo Internacional Natural Park, which is a protected habitat.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Piedras Albas?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for hiking in Piedras Albas. The weather is milder, and the landscape is vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable if hiking during this season. Winters are usually mild, but checking the weather forecast is always recommended.

Q: Are there any notable wildlife or birdwatching opportunities?
A: Absolutely. The area, especially near the River Erjas and within the Tajo Internacional Natural Park, is a significant ecological zone. It's a Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA), offering excellent birdwatching opportunities for species like the black stork, Iberian imperial eagle, and black vulture. Otters can also be spotted near the rivers.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Piedras Albas?
A: The hiking routes in Piedras Albas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from 12 reviews. Hikers often praise the tranquil dehesa woodlands, the unique granite formations, and the well-maintained paths that offer a blend of natural beauty and historical interest.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or landmarks near the hiking areas?
A: Yes, several historical and natural landmarks are close by. You can visit the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara Roman Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, a marvel of ancient engineering. Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6226522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roco-Campofrío Palace&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5582900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Almocóvar Church&lt;/a&gt; in Alcántara, offering cultural insights alongside your outdoor adventures.

Q: Are there places to rest or find water along the trails?
A: Throughout the dehesa, you can find various historical wells such as Pozo Rojo, El Pocito, and Pozo de la Dehesa. Additionally, restored fountains like Fuente de La Casa del Guarda and Fuente Macacha offer refreshing resting points for trekkers. It's still advisable to carry sufficient water, especially on longer routes.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of hikes in Piedras Albas?
A: The trails around Piedras Albas cater to a range of abilities. You'll find a good mix of easy walks, moderate excursions, and a few more challenging routes. The majority of the 14 available routes are classified as easy or moderate, making the area accessible for most hikers.
tips:
On the Spanish side, the bridge is located in the municipality of Alcántara in the province of Cáceres. It is reached via the regional highway EX-207 from the nearby town of Piedras Albas.
On the Portuguese side, it connects with a local road to reach the town of Segura, from which this bridge takes its name.
It was built in the Roman province of Lusitania to give continuity to the Roman road that connected Norba Caesarina (on the Via de la Plata) with the civitas Egitania (Idanha-a-Velha, Portugal) and with Bracara Augusta (Braga, Portugal), in order to save the Erjas river. This river marks the border line between Spain and Portugal, and the bridge serves as an international crossing between both countries.

La Peña Buraca de Alcántara is already an Asset of Cultural Interest in Extremadura with the category of archaeological zone. La Peña Buraca, also known as Canchal de los Ojos, is the most representative element of this enclave that, even though it is very close to Piedras Albas, belongs to the municipality of Alcántara.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from Piedras Albas, which is a common access point for visiting the Puente de Segura. You can usually find parking within the village of Piedras Albas.

faqs.question: How difficult is this hike?
faqs.answer: This is classified as a moderate hike. While it's manageable for most regular hikers, some sections might involve varied paths or rocky areas, especially near the Erjas River.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain around the Segura International Bridge is characterized by the Erjas River cutting through local slate rock. This suggests paths along the river or through rocky areas. Generally, the region offers varied paths, but for this moderate route, expect a mix of natural trails.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Segura International Bridge, a historic Roman bridge over the Erjas River, which marks the border between Spain and Portugal. You might also encounter the Peña Buraca (Canchal de los Ojos) and Medieval Tombs, offering historical and natural interest.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the "Puente de Segura from Piedras Albas" is designed as a round trip, meaning it's a circular walk that brings you back to your starting point in Piedras Albas.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in natural areas to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. Please ensure you carry water for your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Extremadura region can get very hot in summer. Spring (March-May) and Autumn (September-November) typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking, with milder weather and beautiful natural scenery.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Tajo Internacional park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or to access the general area within the Tajo Internacional Natural Park.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For a moderate hike of this length, it's advisable to bring sufficient water, snacks, appropriate hiking footwear, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and layers of clothing suitable for the weather conditions. A map or GPS device is also recommended.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Cañada Real de Gata, the Via de la Estrella, and the Cañada Real de Gata - Cordel de Alcantara.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Piedras Albas, being the starting point, is a small village where you might find local establishments like cafes or bars to refresh yourself before or after your hike. It's always a good idea to check their operating hours in advance.

waypoints.name: Peña Buraca (Canchal de los Ojos) and Medieval Tombs
